title: Harden preset and playlist truth
summary: 0.6.19 keeps the 0.6.18 frame-drop posture, but tightens preset startup, single-file playlist behavior, focus-loss resume capture, and hosted image/startup truth so the player stops silently reusing stale playlist state.
changes: Turn loop and shuffle defaults back off for new standalone player records.
Change preset startup behavior so chooser selection stays neutral until the operator explicitly picks a preset, and default Load Preset On Select back to off.
Stop expanding a single saved file into a sibling playlist on the hosted path, and let preset loads explicitly replace old playlist state instead of reusing overlapping entries.
Capture playback time immediately on app focus loss and pause the VideoPlayer before the focus generation flips.
Commit loaded still images immediately so hosted image surfaces do not wait for a later refresh tick to become truthful.
Extend hosted autostart retries to give authored host surfaces more time to appear before the plugin gives up.
Stamp version 0.6.19 into player.version.json and BuildRuntimeInfo.cs.
Build the 0.6.19 player runtime and refresh the raw dev_deploy baseline player and modern_tv hosted proof artifacts.
reasoning: The current regression packet points more strongly at playlist and preset authority drift than at transport or decode alone.
A saved single file should not silently become a whole sibling playlist again, especially on the hosted path where the operator expects explicit selection truth.
App-switch recovery needs a snapshot before focus loss, not just a later tick trying to infer where playback had been.
operatorVisibleOutcomes: Saved presets should stop reasserting stale playlist state on startup or after overlapping instance loads.
Single-file loads should behave like single-file loads unless the operator explicitly asked for a playlist or directory.
Movie app switches should be less likely to jump back to the beginning, and still-image loads should bind more truthfully without a plugin reload.
knownIssues: If hosted startup still occasionally needs a plugin reload after 0.6.19, the next seam is likely the authored host contract arrival timing rather than preset or playlist truth.
Video performance under heavier multi-instance stress may still need additional work beyond this stability packet.
